behaviors.description: The generated reply references specific points from the incoming email rather than producing a generic response.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the LLM’s output quality, not just local parsing. A mocked generator could pass while a real model still produces generic text, so MOCK-HIDE applies. No multi-step agent chain is required for this behavior alone.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real draft-generation model on representative email threads and assert the draft contains concrete references to thread-specific details (e.g., named requests, dates, prior decisions) above a defined relevance threshold.

behaviors.description: The factual consistency check flags claims in the draft that contradict entries in the knowledge base.
behaviors.justification: This failure appears at module handoff: draft claim extraction + KB retrieval + contradiction logic. It does not inherently require real OS/network/subprocess, and can be caught with real local modules wired together. Agentic is possible if checker is model-based, but not required by the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the real fact-check pipeline components to a local KB fixture and feed drafts with known contradictory claims; verify contradictions are flagged with correct claim-to-KB evidence mapping.

behaviors.description: When the knowledge base has no relevant entries, the draft includes a disclaimer rather than fabricating facts.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on model behavior under missing-evidence context (LLM-DEP). Mocking generation would hide hallucination failures. This is primarily one agentic generation behavior, not a full multi-step agent pipeline requirement.
behaviors.test_description: With KB retrieval returning no relevant entries, invoke the real model and verify outputs include the configured uncertainty/disclaimer language and do not introduce unsupported factual assertions across repeated runs.

behaviors.description: The approval workflow blocks sending until the user explicitly approves — no draft is sent automatically.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is coordination between workflow state, approval gate, and send module. A unit test on one function can miss cross-module race/state bugs. No real external API is required to prove gating logic, so System is not the minimum.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real approval-state and send-orchestrator modules together with a test double outbound sender; assert zero send calls before explicit approval and exactly one send after approval event.

behaviors.description: The full sequence — read thread, generate draft, check facts, present for approval — works end-to-end for a real email thread.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly requires multiple steps in sequence and includes at least one agentic step (generation), so MULTI-STEP + LLM-DEP applies. Lower levels would miss orchestration failures across the full pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the production-like pipeline on a real email thread fixture through all stages (ingest, generation with real model, fact-check, approval UI/state gate) and verify the final approved draft is consistent with stage outputs and only send-enabled after approval.
